body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Primitive

Model metadata reporting: the agent's transcript records the active model name in a field the parser can extract, so the dashboard shows the launch model rather than 'unknown', and the context window matches the model's published window. The capability key is `multi_model_orchestration` in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` (already `true`) â€” aider's first-class model selection is the surface that puts the launch model into the transcript.

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it

Aider is launched with a specific model via `--model <name>` (or the default config model), documented on the official model-selection page (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html and https://aider.chat/docs/llms.html). On the first assistant block of every session aider writes a `> Model: <name> with <edit format>` blockquote into `.aider.chat.history.md` â€” confirmed in committed recordings, e.g. `replaydata/agents/aider/regression/multi-turn-conversation/transcript.md:10` (`> Model: openai/google/gemma-4-26b-a4b with whole edit format`). So the launch model is recorded explicitly in the transcript from turn one.

### irrlicht observes it

The scenario's two user-observable signals are:
1. `ModelName = <launch model>` (not `"unknown"`).
2. `ContextWindow` matches the model's published window.

Both resolve through the aider transport as it exists today:

- **ModelName.** The parser's `modelRE` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go:66`) matches the `> Model: <name>` blockquote, captures the name, runs it through `tailer.NormalizeModelName`, sets `p.model`, and emits a `Skip` event carrying `ModelName` (parser.go:110-113). Every subsequent `> Tokens:` line then stamps that same `p.model` onto each `assistant_message` (`closeModelCall`, parser.go:153-187). The tailer's `applyModelMetadata` (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer_metrics.go:110`) sets `t.metrics.ModelName` on any event with a non-empty `ModelName`, including the `Skip` event â€” so `SessionMetrics.ModelName` is populated from the first assistant block and is never `"unknown"` for a model aider actually names. Transport is `FilesUnderCWD{ .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser }` (`agent.go:45`), a real local transcript, so there is no transport gap of the cloud-only kind.
- **ContextWindow.** Unlike codex (which writes `payload.model_context_window`) or claudecode (`raw.context_management.context_window`), aider's markdown transcript carries NO context-window field â€” the parser never sets `parsed.ContextWindow`, so `contextWindowOverride` stays 0. `ContextWindow` is therefore derived entirely from the capacity manager: `ComputeContextUtilization` falls through to `capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(modelName).ContextWindow` (`tailer_metrics.go:453-454`), i.e. exactly the scenario's third resolution path ("the LiteLLM alias-table fallback"). For any model present in the LiteLLM table (after the `modelAliases` rewrite in `core/pkg/capacity/capacity.go:104` and `aliases.go`), `ContextWindow` is the model's published window. For a model absent from LiteLLM, `GetModelCapacity` returns a zero window and the tailer sets `ContextWindowUnknown: true` (`tailer_metrics.go:457-458, 487`) â€” see caveats.

So both signals fall out of the parser + capacity manager for any LiteLLM-known launch model. The `model_present: true` and basic-turn state arc (`readyâ†’workingâ†’ready`, `tool_calls_max: 0`) in the scenario verify block are satisfied by a single no-op turn (the `> Tokens:` line plus `IdleFlush`-synthesized `turn_done`, parser.go:216).

### Matrix drift

The coverage matrix already records `agent_supports: yes`, `irrlicht_observes: yes` with the note "regex-parsed '> Model:' / '> Main model:'". This assessment ratifies that verdict â€” no drift.

## Confidence

0.85: the `> Model:` line is documented and visible in committed aider recordings, and the ModelName path is grounded in the adapter source plus pinning tests (`parser_test.go`). Held below 0.9 because `ContextWindow` correctness depends on the launch model being present in the LiteLLM capacity table â€” true for mainstream models (claude-*, gpt-5) but not for the exotic/local gemma models aider's existing fixtures happen to use, and no end-to-end recording of this specific cell exists yet (that is Stage 4's job).
caveats: ContextWindow correctness is contingent on the LiteLLM capacity table: aider's markdown transcript carries no context-window field, so the value comes solely from `capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(modelName)`. A model absent from LiteLLM (e.g. the local/exotic `openai/gemma-*` models used in aider's current fixtures) resolves to a zero window with `ContextWindowUnknown: true` â€” the ModelName is still correct, but the published-window assertion only holds for LiteLLM-known launch models.

The `> Weak model: â€¦` line aider may also print is intentionally NOT matched by `modelRE` (parser.go:65) â€” only `Model:` / `Main model:` drive `ModelName`, which is correct here since the weak/summary model is not the turn's main model.

sources.note: official model-selection docs: aider launches against a specific model via --model / config; the chosen model is the Main Model recorded in the session

modelRE (L66) matches `> Model: <name>`; sets p.model via NormalizeModelName and emits Skip event with ModelName (L110-113); closeModelCall (L153) stamps p.model on each assistant_message; parser never sets ContextWindow

applyModelMetadata (L110) sets metrics.ModelName from any non-empty ModelName event incl. the Skip event; ComputeContextUtilization (L447-458) derives ContextWindow from capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity when no override, sets ContextWindowUnknown when the model is absent
GetModelCapacity (L101) applies modelAliases then looks up the LiteLLM table; returns zero ContextWindow for unknown models â€” the alias-table fallback path the scenario names
L10 shows the real first-assistant `> Model: openai/google/gemma-4-26b-a4b with whole edit format` blockquote aider writes â€” confirms the line shape modelRE parses
multi_model_orchestration: true (the capability key for this primitive); headless_mode: true (the scenario's requires:)
